title: single cell RNA sequencing of hashimoto's thyroiditis
author: Huai-dong Song
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Hashimoto’s thyroiditis is the most common autoimmune disease characterized by lymphocytic infiltration and thyrocyte destruction. However, the pathogenesis of HT is not clear. Here, we analyzed 50,535 cells from thyroid tissues and 48,822 cells from peripheral blood mononuclear cells of 5 HT patients at single cell resolution.
steps_raw: Hashimoto’s thyroiditis is the most common autoimmune disease characterized by lymphocytic infiltration and thyrocyte destruction. However, the pathogenesis of HT is not clear. Here, we analyzed 50,535 cells from thyroid tissues and 48,822 cells from peripheral blood mononuclear cells of 5 HT patients at single cell resolution.
